key_points: Male housing options severely limited compared to female housing
Kennedy Hall and VC Hall are popular; quad layouts confuse incoming students
No clear info on room dimensions or layouts on official website
key_quotes: I look on Hampton's website and see men's housing has like 6 houses compared to the girls
I got a quad dorm in the VC Hall but have no idea what it looks like
I can't find [room dimensions] anywhere on their website or anywhere else on Google

key_points: Greek life dominates campus culture; non-D9 students can feel excluded
Not a party school — described as structured and dress-code enforced
Strong community and HBCU culture but cliquish if you're not legacy or Greek
key_quotes: STAY THE HELL OFF OGDEN CIRCLE! DO NOT PUT SO MUCH AS A TOENAIL ON THAT GRASS!!!
I wasn't getting the level of education I wanted and the people there were not friendly
Hampton shapes our identity. We know who we are and come out capable

key_points: Food quality has been a long-standing complaint across multiple decades
One student added salt to food for the first time in their life at Hampton
Improvement noted over time, but cafeteria still draws criticism
key_quotes: Coming from Houston, Hampton is the first time I ever added salt to my food
Gourmet services...grade e but edible. That's what the box said when I snuck in the kitchen
HIU has come a LONG way since the big caf' in VC in the mid 90's!

key_points: Health center turned away a sick student — offered ice instead of treatment
Medical neglect cited as a primary reason one student transferred out
No strong positive data on counseling or mental health support services
key_quotes: When you touched on the medical reasoning, I was with your decision 100 percent
I would be highly upset if my daughter was turned away from medical treatment of something serious, and for ice
I didn't feel like I was a part of anything... I left after my 1st semester

key_points: Financial aid office is notoriously unresponsive — multiple students report no callbacks
Full rides are possible with strong stats (3.3+ GPA, 1000+ SAT)
Cost has nearly doubled since early 2000s; now ~$40K/year with fees on top
key_quotes: I called the financial aid office three separate times and nobody was available
Hampton cost $40K now?!?! It was 25K back in '04 and I left with over $100K in student loans smh
Howard offered me 20k out of their 58k. Tuskegee offered me a full ride. Hampton offered me a full ride. Apply everywhere!

key_points: Hampton listed among HBCUs with graduation rates above 50% — a meaningful distinction
Journalism/Mass Media curriculum seen as thin by prospective students
Described as one of the top underrated schools nationally for academics
key_quotes: If you can effectively and efficiently deal with Hampton University, then you can HANDLE ANYTHING!
When I looked at the curriculum, it seemed like there were only a few classes
Hampton University [is] underrated per U.S. News rankings

key_points: Admissions office hard to reach; multiple prospective students report no response
Scholarship portal errors and password reset failures go unresolved
Student rebellion reported in grad school; administration slow to respond to concerns
key_quotes: The admissions office is very hard to get into contact with
I have emailed several times and have received no response
I went to Hampton for grad school. Shit was terrible. I was so disappointed in my people. I'm glad the students finally rebelled this year

key_points: Campus is widely praised as one of the most beautiful HBCUs in the country
Waterfront location and historic buildings are a genuine draw
Private campus — non-students have restricted access to some areas
key_quotes: Hampton University is by far one of the most beautiful college campuses in North America
I miss those days studying by the waterfront with my crew
It's private — outside some areas you have to be a student or staff

key_points: Alumni describe being 'polished and prepared' for professional environments
Business school outcomes questioned by at least one parent comparing to PWIs
Sports marketing and journalism tracks exist but depth of programming is unclear
key_quotes: HU alum are polished and prepared. We know how to carry ourselves
He's been accepted at some other non-HBCU schools that he thinks will better serve him as a business major
Being a Hampton graduate is the best thing you can be

key_points: At $40K/year, students report still paying extra for basic necessities
Multiple students and parents say comparable HBCU experiences exist for less money
Full rides are available but require strong test scores submitted at application
key_quotes: Damn why does it cost $40,000 if you literally have to pay for EVERYTHING... what exactly does the tuition cover?
I felt it was too expensive for what you're getting out of it... I could get way more FOR LESS somewhere else
Even though I was on an academic scholarship, stayed in the new honors dorm and with a 3.7 GPA the school still wasn't for me

key_points: Strong HBCU pride and identity — Hampton shapes who you become
Dress codes and formal culture are real; not a casual campus experience
Legacy students and Greek-affiliated families dominate the social atmosphere
key_quotes: I love HU and am very concerned. Stay strong, stay vigilant, my brothers and sisters. You attend a great institution that is the envy of many
Everybody is Jack and Jill, entire family D9, 4th generation student — it makes me not want to be here
This is my 'Home by the Sea' — forever a Hamptonian

key_points: **Health Center:** Student turned away while ill; offered ice instead of care
**Administration:** Financial aid office unreachable; portal errors left unresolved for weeks
**Cost vs. Value:** $40K/year tuition with additional out-of-pocket costs reported by students on scholarships
key_quotes: I was shocked to hear that so many things haven't changed — I transferred after my first semester back in 2001
This is the most annoying thing ever because the office isn't even responding — this is miserable
Wish I never went to Hampton [left with $100K+ in loans]

key_points: Full rides genuinely available — one student received them from both Hampton and Tuskegee
Honda Campus All-Star Challenge powerhouse — Hampton reached finals in academic quiz bowl
Virginia lawmakers seeking to restore land-grant status, which could unlock more scholarship funding
key_quotes: My son just got a full ride to Hampton University! Child #2 will graduate debt free!
Congratulations to Hampton for making it to the Great 8 at the Honda Campus All-Star challenge
VA lawmakers seek to restore Hampton University's land-grant status after a century
